name: Circular route from Buñol crossing the Mijares River
name_original: Recorrido circular desde Buñol cruzando el Río Mijares
region_intro: Road cycling around Buñol offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain in the Valencian Community of Spain. The region features rolling hills, mountainous areas with pine-tree-covered slopes, and distant peaks. Cyclists can navigate routes that include both flat sections and challenging climbs, often passing by natural water features like the Juanes River. The lush Valencian landscapes provide a scenic backdrop for road cycling routes. Best road cycling routes around Buñol The most popular road cycling route is The Embalse de Forata - Circular from Buñol, a 48.1 miles (77.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Circular route from Buñol crossing the Mijares River, a difficult 50.2 miles (80.9 km) path. This route offers extensive views and crosses the Mijares River. Local road cyclists also love the Fuente Real Pass – View of Dos Aguas loop from Buñol, a 65.6 miles (105.5 km) trail leading through mountainous areas and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 5 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Buñol is defined by rolling hills, mountainous terrain, and natural water features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Buñol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Buñol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Buñol?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Buñol, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult options to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Buñol?
A: Road cycling routes in Buñol feature a varied and engaging terrain. You can expect rolling hills, mountainous areas with pine-tree-covered slopes, and distant peaks. Routes often include both flat sections and challenging climbs, all set within lush Valencian landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Buñol?
A: The routes around Buñol are generally suited for cyclists with some experience. Out of over 40 routes, 16 are classified as moderate and 24 as difficult, often featuring significant elevation gains. There are no easy routes listed, indicating a focus on more challenging rides.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or landmarks to see along the road cycling routes in Buñol?
A: Yes, many routes pass by notable natural features. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/331736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turche Cave Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/331737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mañán Pool&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4405761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Doves and Yátova Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The Juanes River is also integrated into the natural environment of many cycling paths.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Absolutely. Buñol is known for its stunning natural water features. Routes often lead past attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/331736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turche Cave Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be particularly impressive after heavy rains, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2638202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful pool on the Juanes River&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a scenic stop.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Buñol offers several challenging long-distance routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38179174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Real Pass – View of Dos Aguas loop from Buñol&lt;/a&gt; covers over 105 km with significant elevation gain, typically taking around 5 hours 18 minutes to complete. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24530197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca / Cueva del Turche – Mijares River loop from Buñol&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 93.5 km.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Buñol?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Buñol are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e853397435/the-embalse-de-forata-circular-from-bunol" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Embalse de Forata - Circular from Buñol&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 77.4 km route, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e853342781/circular-route-from-bunol-crossing-the-mijares-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route from Buñol crossing the Mijares River&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 80.9 km.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Buñol?
A: Buñol, located in the Valencian Community, generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, while summer can be hot, especially during midday. Winter is typically mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though cooler temperatures and occasional rain should be expected.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Buñol?
A: While many routes around Buñol are classified as moderate to difficult due to elevation changes, the region's extensive network means there are likely flatter, less challenging sections or local roads suitable for families. It's advisable to check individual route profiles for specific details on distance and elevation to find the most suitable options.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Buñol?
A: The road cycling routes in Buñol are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the varied terrain that includes both climbs and scenic descents, and the natural beauty of the Valencian region, including its water features and pine forests.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes for those looking for a less intense ride?
A: Yes, while many routes are difficult, there are 16 routes classified as moderate. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10409761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir of the Magre River – Bar El Cazador loop from Buñol&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 66.6 km with a more manageable elevation gain, typically completed in around 3 hours.
tips: From the road you cross the bridge over the Mijares River, a very colorful and picturesque point. In summer it can be a good place to take a refreshing swim. From here you can connect with the GR-7 paths or the paved secondary road that reaches the town of Mijares.
